summaryText: Pastor Todd opened the service on Independence Day by welcoming the congregation and online viewers, connecting the national holiday to the spiritual freedom found in Christ. He reflected on the historical acknowledgment of God in America's founding documents and the biblical call to freedom. The central message, titled 'Freedom is Calling,' addressed three areas where believers often need deliverance: the pain from our past, the pressures of our present, and the fear of our future. Using personal stories and biblical examples like Job, the woman with the issue of blood, and Joseph, Pastor Todd emphasized that God desires to set His people free from burdens, grief, and bondage they were never meant to carry. He taught that freedom requires a decision to trust God, an acknowledgment of pain and responsibility, and surrounding oneself with positive, faith-filled people. The sermon concluded with a powerful call to prayer, inviting those struggling with secret burdens or needing salvation to receive God's touch and commit to living in the freedom Christ purchased.
mainPoints: We must find freedom from the pain of our past by deciding to trust God, acknowledging our pain, and refusing to remain a victim.
We must navigate the pressures of our present by trusting God's process, focusing on His provision, and not longing for past bondage.
We must overcome the fear of our future by trusting that God holds our destiny and can turn intended harm into good for His purpose.

versesMentioned.text: It is for freedom that Christ has set us free. Stand firm, then, and do not let yourselves be burdened again by a yoke of slavery.
I have no peace, no quietness; I have no rest, but only turmoil.

No temptation has overtaken you except what is common to mankind. And God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, he will also provide a way out so that you can endure it.

The Spirit of the Sovereign LORD is on me, because the LORD has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives and release from darkness for the prisoners.

Therefore, say to the Israelites: ‘I am the LORD, and I will bring you out from under the yoke of the Egyptians. I will free you from being slaves to them, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with mighty acts of judgment. I will take you as my own people, and I will be your God. Then you will know that I am the LORD your God, who brought you out from under the yoke of the Egyptians. And I will bring you to the land I swore with uplifted hand to give to Abraham, to Isaac and to Jacob. I will give it to you as a possession. I am the LORD.’
The righteous person may have many troubles, but the LORD delivers him from them all.
For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid, but gives us power, love and self-discipline.
Jesus replied, “You do not realize now what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives.
See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and streams in the desert.

Monday.scripture: Galatians 5:1 (NIV) - 'It is for freedom that Christ has set us free. Stand firm, then, and do not let yourselves be burdened again by a yoke of slavery.'
Monday.focus: The foundational truth of your freedom in Christ.
Monday.action: Identify one 'yoke' or burden you have picked back up. Write it down and pray, releasing it to Jesus.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for setting me free. Help me to stand firm today and not take back the burdens you carried for me.

Tuesday.scripture: Job 3:26 (NIV) - 'I have no peace, no quietness; I have no rest, but only turmoil.'
Tuesday.focus: Acknowledging the pain and unrest from your past.
Tuesday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet reflection. If a painful memory surfaces, tell God about it simply, without rehearsing the details.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I bring my turmoil to you. You see my pain. I ask for your peace and rest to fill the places where I have none.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 10:13 (NIV) - '...God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear...'
Wednesday.focus: God's faithfulness in providing a way through present pressure.
Wednesday.action: When you feel overwhelmed today, pause and ask: 'God, what is the way out you have provided?' Look for one practical step.
Wednesday.prayer: Faithful God, I trust that you see my struggle. Open my eyes to the way out you have prepared for me today.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 61:1 (NIV) - '...to proclaim freedom for the captives and release from darkness for the prisoners...'
Thursday.focus: Your purpose to bring freedom to others.
Thursday.action: Perform one small, anonymous act of kindness for someone, seeing it as an act of 'proclaiming freedom' through love.
Thursday.prayer: Anointed Savior, let your Spirit work through me today. Use my life as a platform to bring a taste of your freedom to someone else.

Friday.scripture: Genesis 50:20 (NIV) - 'You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good...'
Friday.focus: God's power to redeem your past for a good future.
Friday.action: Reflect on a past hurt. Write down one way God could potentially use that experience for good, even if you don't see it yet.
Friday.prayer: Redeeming God, I choose to believe you can work all things for good. I release my need for vengeance and trust you with my story.

Saturday.scripture: Isaiah 43:19 (NIV) - 'See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it?'
Saturday.focus: Anticipating God's new work and letting go of the old.
Saturday.action: Declutter one small physical space in your home (a drawer, a shelf) as a tangible act of making room for God's 'new thing.'
Saturday.prayer: God of new beginnings, open my eyes to perceive the new thing you are doing. I let go of the old and make room for your fresh work in my life.
